title: Reptiles
description: Learn reptiles in Filipino Sign Language

pages.title: Welcome to Reptiles
pages.content: In this lesson, you'll learn how to sign reptiles in Filipino Sign Language (FSL). You'll learn 5 important signs with step-by-step instructions and practice questions.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Snake'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Snake'. A long, legless reptile that moves by gliding on its belly. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'bent V' handshape (middle and index bent). 2. Move the hand forward in a serpentine (wiggling) motion.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Lizard'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Lizard'. A reptile typically having a long body, four legs, and a tapering tail. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'L' handshape. 2. Move the hand in a crawling motion up the non-dominant forearm.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Turtle'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Turtle'. A slow-moving reptile encased in a shell. 

How to sign:
1. Use the non-dominant hand as a 'dome' (shell). 2. Use the dominant 'A' hand (head/neck). 3. Tuck the 'A' hand under the dome, then move it forward and out.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Crocodile'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Crocodile'. A large predatory semiaquatic reptile with a long jaw and powerful tail. 

How to sign:
1. Use both hands in the '5' handshape, held flat and stacked at the wrist. 2. Open and close the hands like a large jaw.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Alligator'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Alligator'. A large reptile with a shorter, blunter snout than a crocodile. 

How to sign:
1. Same sign as Crocodile.

pages.title: Practice: Snake
pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.

question.explanation:

How to sign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'bent V' handshape (middle and index bent). 2. Move the hand forward in a serpentine (wiggling) motion.
